note: 43 students from Ayotzinapa Normal School forcibly taken on the night of Sep 26–27, 2014. Classified as enforced disappearances. Fate of 40 remains officially unknown as of 2026.

Six people killed: students Aldo Gutiérrez Solano (survived in vegetative state until 2019), Julio César Mondragón Fontes (face flayed), Daniel Solís Gallardo, Julio César Ramírez Nava, and Víctor Manuel Lugo Ortiz; plus bystander Blanca Montiel Sánchez.
At least 25 people injured including students, bystanders, and Avispones football team members. Aldo Gutiérrez Solano was shot in the head and remained in a vegetative state until his death in 2019.
The Avispones de Chilpancingo youth football team bus was also attacked that night, killing 3 players and injuring others. The attack raised questions about the scope of the operation.

Three students positively identified: Alexander Mora Venancio (Dec 2014), Christian Alfonso Rodríguez Telumbre (2020), and Jhosivani Guerrero de la Cruz (2022). The first identification was later found to involve a bone fragment allegedly planted by investigator Tomás Zerón.

category: Suspects Arrested in Connection with Case
note: More than 142 individuals arrested between 2014 and 2024. Many convictions overturned due to torture-tainted confessions. Includes local police officers, Guerreros Unidos cartel members, Mayor Abarca, former AG Murillo Karam, and General Rodríguez Pérez. Significant number acquitted or released.
Student Aldo Gutiérrez Solano was shot in the head on Sep 26, 2014, and spent years in a vegetative state before dying in 2019. His case exemplified the long-term human cost of the attack.
